content.cn_text: 大家下午好。
content.en_text: Good afternoon, everyone.

content.cn_text: 我在这里给大家讲一下租赁协议和其他法律问题。
content.en_text: I'm here to give you a talk on tenancy agreements and other legalities.

content.cn_text: 如果你租了一套公寓或房子，你就会有一份“租赁协议”或“租约”。
content.en_text: If you rent a flat or a house, or bedsit, you will have a 'tenancy agreement' or 'lease'.

content.cn_text: 这是你(租客)和业主(房东)之间的书面法律协议。
content.en_text: This is a written legal agreement between you, the tenant, and the property owner, the landlord.

content.cn_text: 租约通常应载有租金金额、租约期限，以及你和业主在法律下享有的权利。
content.en_text: The tenancy agreements should normally contain information about the amount of rent,the length of the tenancy and what rights you and your landlord will have under the law.

content.cn_text: 在大多数情况下，你将有一个“保证短期租赁”，这意味着你的房东不能要求你在前六个月内没有充分的理由离开。
content.en_text: In most cases, you will have an 'assured short-hold tenancy' which means that your landlord cannot ask you to leave without a good reason during the first six months.

content.cn_text: 虽然这些权利为您提供了一些保护，但您仍然有责任彻底检查租赁协议并确保您同意条款。
content.en_text: Although these rights offer you some protection, it is still your responsibility to check the tenancy agreement thoroughly and make sure you agree with the terms.

content.cn_text: 如果你不知道租约的全部含义，不要签署租约。
content.en_text: Do not sign the tenancy agreement if you do not know what all of it means.

content.cn_text: 如果你不完全了解你的权利，向你所在大学或学院的住宿办公室或学生福利办公室的顾问出示你的租约，并寻求帮助。
content.en_text: If you do not fully understand your rights, show your tenancy agreement to an advisor in the accommodation office or student welfare office at your university or college and ask for help.

content.cn_text: 你也可以向房屋谘询中心、法律中心或市民谘询局寻求帮助。
content.en_text: You can also get help from a housing advice centre, law centre or citizens advice Bureau.

content.cn_text: 当你签署租赁协议时，确保你有一份自己的副本，以防你以后需要检查任何细节。
content.en_text: When you do sign the tenancy agreement, make sure you get a copy to keep for yourself, in case you need to check any details later on.

content.cn_text: 房东也可能要求你签署一份清单:一份财产中所有物品的清单，如家具、厨房用品等。
content.en_text: The landlord may also, ask you to sign an inventory: a list of all the items in the property – pieces of furniture, kitchen items, etc.

content.cn_text: 如果是这样，确保你也有一份副本。
content.en_text: If so, make sure you get a copy of this as well.

content.cn_text: 在签署合同之前，请检查合同是否正确，是否包括这些物品的任何损坏。
content.en_text: Check that it is correct and that any existing damage to these items is included before signing it.

content.cn_text: 如果你的房东没有提供清单，你应该自己制作一份，然后寄给房东一份。
content.en_text: If your landlord does not provide an inventory, you should make one yourself and send a copy to the landlord.

content.cn_text: 让我们看一下付款情况。
content.en_text: Let's take a look at payments.

content.cn_text: 在你搬进私人公寓之前，你可能会被要求支付相当于一个月租金的押金。
content.en_text: Before you move into private accommodation, you will probably be asked to pay a deposit equivalent to one month's rent.

content.cn_text: 确保你有一张你所支付的任何押金或费用的收据。
content.en_text: Make sure you get a receipt for any deposits or fees you have paid.

content.cn_text: 当你离开住宿时，如果你已经支付了所有的账单，并且没有对财产造成损害，你的全部押金将被退还给你。
content.en_text: When you leave the accommodation, if you have paid all your bills and caused no damage to the property, your full deposit will be paid back to you.

content.cn_text: 如果你是通过住宿中介租房，你可能还需要支付准备租赁协议和管理的费用。
content.en_text: If you are renting through an accommodation agency, you may also be asked to pay fees for preparing tenancy agreements and administration.

content.cn_text: 你还应该保留一份书面记录，记录下你支付的所有租金。
content.en_text: You should also keep a written record of all the rent payments that you make, as you make them.

content.cn_text: 如果你和房东有纠纷，或者你拖欠房租，你应该尽快得到建议。
content.en_text: If you have a dispute with your landlord, or you get behind with your rent, you should get advice as soon as possible.

content.cn_text: 记住，如果你
content.en_text: Remember that if you

content.cn_text: 和你的房东住在同一栋楼里，或者你在学生或青年旅馆或大学/学院宿舍里有一个房间，那么这将影响你的租赁安全程度。
content.en_text: live in the same building as your landlord, or you have a room in a student or youth hostel, or university/college accommodation, then this will affect how secure your tenancy will be.

content.cn_text: 如果你不与房东或其家庭成员共享任何生活空间，除了入口大厅或楼梯等进出方式，或者你是住在学生宿舍或任何其他类型的住宿，教育机构是房东，你将得到基本的保护，不被驱逐。
content.en_text: If you do not share any living space with the landlord or a member of their family, apart from means of access like an entrance hall or a staircase, or are a student living in halls of residence, or any other type of accommodation where an educational institution is the landlord, you will have basic protection from eviction.

content.cn_text: 你的房东必须先终止你的租约，要么等待你同意的固定租期结束，要么给你至少四周的书面通知，要求你退出，要么在你必须离开之前获得法院命令。
content.en_text: Your landlord will have to end your tenancy first, either by waiting for the end of the fixed term you agreed for the tenancy or by giving you at least four weeks' written notice in writing to quit  or through getting a court order before you have to leave.

content.cn_text: 如果你和房东共用一个生活空间，比如厨房、客厅或浴室，或者你住在学生或青年旅馆里，你就会成为所谓的“被排除在外的房客”，这意味着你不受英国法律的保护，不受英国法律对租赁的保护，也不会有租赁的保障。
content.en_text: If you share living space – for example, a kitchen, living room or bathroom – with your landlord or are in a student or youth hostel, you will be what is termed an excluded tenant, which means that you are outside the protection of the UK law which regulates tenancies and will not have security of tenure.

content.cn_text: 房东只需要给你一个通知就可以把你赶出去，不过他们必须给你一个合理的时间让你离开。
content.en_text: All the landlord has to do to evict you is to give you notice, although they must give you a reasonable amount of time in which to leave.

content.cn_text: 如果你在住宿方面有问题，请联系住宿办公室或你所在大学或学院的学生会。
content.en_text: If you have problems with accommodation, contact the accommodation office or students' union at your university or college.

content.cn_text: 如果你需要专家或法律帮助，可以联系当地公民咨询局的法律中心，他们会告诉你作为租客的权利和房东的权利。
content.en_text: If you need specialist or legal help, contact a law centre in your local Citizens Advice Bureau who will be able to tell you your rights as a tenant and the rights of your landlord.

groups.desc:
Questions 11-14
Complete the following sentences using NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS for each gap.
groups.collect:
A tenancy agreement is a $13740$ between the tenant and the landlord.
An assured short-hold tenancy means that your landlord cannot ask you to leave without a good reason during the $13741$
Tenants should keep a $13742$ of the tenancy agreement.
$13743$ is a list of all the items in the property.

items.analysis:
这道题的关键在于准确理解"tenancy agreement"的定义。文中明确指出，租约是一份"written legal agreement"（书面法律协议）。这个定义包含了三个重要元素：1) 它是书面的（written）；2) 它具有法律效力（legal）；3) 它是一个协议（agreement）。这三个元素都必须包含在答案中，因为它们共同构成了租约的完整定义。

其他可能的表述，如只写"legal agreement"或"agreement between tenant and landlord"，虽然部分正确，但都不够完整。"written"这个词很重要，因为它强调了租约必须是书面形式，而不是口头约定。

此外，注意听力中使用了"This is"这个表达来引出定义，这是一个很好的信号词，提示我们接下来会听到重要信息。在做类似的题目时，要特别留意这样的表达方式，它们往往会引出关键的定义或解释。

这道题的关键在于理解"assured short-hold tenancy"（有保障的短期租约）的含义。文中明确指出，这种租约意味着在租约开始的前六个月内，房东不能无故要求租户离开。因此，正确答案是"first six months"（前六个月）。这个答案直接对应了题目中所问的时间段。注意，答案不仅包括时间长度（six months），还包括了这个时间段的具体位置（first）。这体现了雅思听力考试对细节的重视，要求考生不仅要听懂大意，还要准确捕捉具体信息。在做类似的题目时，要特别注意与时间、数字相关的表述，因为这些往往是答案的关键部分。

这道题的关键在于理解租客需要保留什么文件。原文中明确指出，在签署租赁协议时，你应该获得并保留一份副本。这里的"copy"就是答案。文中强调了保留副本的重要性，因为你可能需要在之后查看细节。此外，"copy"这个词直接出现在关键句中，与题目要求的"keep"相呼应。记住，在租房过程中，保留重要文件的副本是非常必要的，这不仅可以保护你的权益，还能在日后出现争议时作为重要证据。因此，"copy"是最合适且唯一正确的答案。

这道题的正确答案是"An inventory"，因为原文中明确指出"inventory"就是"a list of all the items in the property"。这里运用了同义词替换的技巧，将题干中的描述直接对应到了听力材料中的专业术语"inventory"上。作为学生，你要注意听力中出现的与题干描述相似的表达，尤其是在租房这样的特定场景中可能出现的专业词汇。同时，要学会利用上下文信息，如这里提到的家具和厨房用品，来帮助你确定"inventory"的具体含义。记住，在雅思听力中，同义词替换是一个常见的考点，所以平时学习时要多积累同义表达，提高快速识别的能力。

groups.title: Decide which THREE of the following statements about deposits are correct and write the appropriate letters on your answer sheet in any order.

collect_options.title: Decide which THREE of the following statements about deposits are correct and write the appropriate letters on your answer sheet in any order.

list.title: B
list.content: They are generally equal to one months'rent.

list.title: C
list.content: They can be used instead of one months'rent.

list.title: D
list.content: They can cover unpaid bills when you leave accommodation.

list.title: E
list.content: They can cover the cost of damage to furniture.

list.title: F
list.content: They are not payable when you use an accommodation agency.

list.title: G
list.content: They can cover the legal costs of tenancy disputes.

items.analysis: 这道题考察了学生对押金（deposit）概念的理解。正确答案是B、D和E，因为原文明确提到押金通常等于一个月的租金（B），可以用来支付未付的账单（D），以及赔偿对房产造成的损坏（E）。这些信息直接对应了题目中的三个正确选项，体现了押金的金额和主要用途。

groups.title: Decide which are THREE ways a landlord can legally evict a tenant (other than an excluded tenant) and write the appropriate letters on your answer sheet in any order.

collect_options.title: Decide which are THREE ways a landlord can legally evict a tenant (other than an excluded tenant) and write the appropriate letters on your answer sheet in any order.

list.title: B
list.content: By moving a member of his /her family Into the property.

list.title: C
list.content: By applying to your educational institution for your eviction.

list.title: D
list.content: By waiting until the tenancy agreement expires.

list.title: E
list.content: By giving you notice that you have to leave.

list.title: F
list.content: By giving you at least 4 weeks' notice that you have to leave.

list.title: G
list.content: By getting a court to issue an order for you to leave.

list.title: H
list.content: By contacting a law centre or the Citizens' Advice Bureau.

items.analysis: 根据原文，房东合法驱逐租客的三种方式是：等待固定租期结束（D选项），提前至少四周给出书面通知（F选项），以及获得法院命令（G选项）。这三种方式都符合法律程序，保护了租客的权益，同时也给予了房东合法终止租约的途径。
